compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
`_mkdir_lock_is_stale` (`src/rebar/_store/lock.py:169-188`) has branches for read errors
(`except OSError: return False`), missing separator, foreign host, non-int PID
(`except ValueError: return False`), and live/dead PID. Existing tests cover dead/live/
absent/foreign-host, but the **non-int PID, missing-`:`-separator, and owner-file read-error**
branches are untested (PARTIALLY-VALIDATED: a real partial-branch coverage gap). The safety
property is conservative — none of these may cause reclamation.

## What
Add a single table-driven test over the malformed/unreadable owner-stamp forms, asserting
`_mkdir_lock_is_stale` returns `False` (never reclaim) for each. Deterministic realizations of
the two racy cases:
- **unreadable owner file** → put a DIRECTORY at the owner-file path so `open()` raises
  `IsADirectoryError` (an `OSError`) → the `except OSError: return False` branch.
- **owner file replaced/observed mid-write** → a partial stamp with the colon not yet written
  (hostname only), i.e. the exact content a reader that caught the write in flight would see →
  the missing-separator branch → `False`.

## Scope
- `tests/unit/test_lock_stale_reclaim.py` — one parametrized/table-driven test of the
  malformed-stamp forms.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Table covers: empty owner file; missing colon; empty host; empty PID; non-numeric PID;
      negative PID; zero PID; extra delimiters (`host:1:2`); foreign host; a directory in place
      of the owner file (unreadable); and a partial mid-write stamp (colon not yet written).
- [ ] Every case asserts `_mkdir_lock_is_stale(...) is False` — conservative, no reclamation.
- [ ] `make test` green.
compiled_state.title: Add table-driven mkdir-lock malformed ownership-stamp tests
